surfaces.name: Go orchestrator runOnce
surfaces.notes: Claims jobs, builds runtime-service requests, records failures, completes Matches.

surfaces.name: Go job lifecycle
surfaces.notes: Owns claim, lease, heartbeat, retry queue, terminal failure, and failure detail sanitization.

surfaces.name: Go completion service
surfaces.notes: Owns Chronicle persistence handoff, Match completion, and MatchSet refresh under lease.

surfaces.name: Go retry policy
surfaces.notes: Classifies runtime unavailable, timeout, malformed runtime result, stale artifact, and system failure categories.

surfaces.name: Runtime-service client
surfaces.notes: Translates runtime-service HTTP, timeout, malformed, oversized, and contract failure shapes.

surfaces.name: Runtime-service execute-match
surfaces.notes: Owns hostile Strategy execution and schema-validated runtime envelopes only.

surfaces.name: Runtime-service redaction
surfaces.notes: Redacts runtime diagnostics before envelopes leave the runtime boundary.

surfaces.name: v1.26 reliability proof
surfaces.notes: Existing proof pattern for contract fixtures, privacy scans, markers, and non-claims.

gapInventory.summary: No dedicated private operator quarantine model exists for exhausted and non-retryable execution jobs.
Existing run-once supports execution, but explicit idempotent recovery controls are not modeled.
v1.26 proof covers reliability fixtures, but v1.28 needs stateful recovery drills across local topology.
Existing lifecycle tests cover basics; operator recovery needs convergence proof under manual and duplicate paths.
Public redaction exists, but private operator artifacts need their own allowlist and leak scans.
Boundary monitors need v1.28 proof checks for no contract drift, no ownership creep, and no leaks.
